---
title: "Gravitational Waves"
subtitle: "Synthèse du cours de PHYS-F484"
toc: true
---

::: {.callout-warning appearance="minimal" collapse="true"}
## ⚠️ Avertissement concernant ces notes
Les notes publiées sur ce site sont basées sur ma compréhension personnelle du matériel et n'ont pas été indépendamment vérifiées. Bien que j'espère qu'elles soient utiles, il peut y avoir des erreurs ou des inexactitudes. Si vous trouvez des erreurs ou avez des suggestions d'amélioration, n'hésitez pas à me contacter : [a.d@csic.es](mailto:a.d@csic.es).
:::

**Enseignant :** Sébastien Clesse, Nicolas Chamel & Geoffrey Compère (Année 2024 - 2025)  
**Ressources officielles :** [<i class="bi bi-link-45deg"></i> Page de l'ULB](https://www.ulb.be/fr/programme/phys-f484){.btn .btn-outline-light .btn-sm .ms-2}
[<i class="bi bi-folder2-open"></i> Espace Dochub](https://dochub.be/catalog/course/phys-f484){.btn .btn-outline-light .btn-sm .ms-2}

---

## Table des matières

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.p-3 .rounded .shadow-sm style="background-color: var(--card-bg); border: 1px solid var(--border-flat); height: 100%; display: flex; flex-direction: column;"}
### Lecture Notes: Theory

* **General Relativity & GWs**
  * Foundations of gravitational wave theory
  * Linearized gravity and wave equations
  * Multipole expansion and energy loss

[<i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Theory Notes](./assets/GW/GW1.pdf){.btn-surface .c-purple .w-100 style="margin-top: auto; min-height: 40px; height: auto; padding: 8px 12px; font-size: 0.9em;"}
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.p-3 .rounded .shadow-sm style="background-color: var(--card-bg); border: 1px solid var(--border-flat); height: 100%; display: flex; flex-direction: column;"}
### Experimental & Astrophysics Notes

* **Experimental Physics**
  * Detection principles and interferometers
* **Astrophysical Sources**
  * Neutron stars, binary systems, and cosmic events
  * Read additional [Astrophysics notes](./assets/GW/PHYS-F-484_B_16439_spl.pdf)

[<i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Experimental Notes](./assets/GW/GW_phys-f-484_part_Clesse.pdf){.btn-surface .c-purple .w-100 style="margin-top: auto; min-height: 40px; height: auto; padding: 8px 12px; font-size: 0.9em;"}
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.p-3 .rounded .shadow-sm style="background-color: var(--card-bg); border: 1px solid var(--border-flat); height: 100%; display: flex; flex-direction: column;"}
### Problem Sets & Corrections

* **Problem Set 1 & 2**
  * Quasi-circular Inspirals ([Correction](./assets/GW/TPs/TP1_ Quasi-circular Inspirals.pdf))
  * GWs from Eccentric Binaries ([Correction](./assets/GW/TPs/TP2_ GWs from Eccentric Binaries.pdf))
* **Problem Set 3 & 4**
  * Quasi-Normal Modes Part 1 ([Correction](./assets/GW/TPs/TP3_ QNMs and Schwarzschild.pdf))
  * Quasi-Normal Modes Part 2 ([PDF](./assets/GW/TPs/TP4_QNMs_II.pdf))

[<i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Problem Set 1](./assets/GW/TPs/TP1 - Quasi-circular inspirals.pdf){.btn-surface .c-purple .w-100 style="margin-top: auto; min-height: 40px; height: auto; padding: 8px 12px; font-size: 0.9em;"}
:::
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
::: {.p-3 .rounded .shadow-sm style="background-color: var(--card-bg); border: 1px solid var(--border-flat); height: 100%; display: flex; flex-direction: column;"}
### Personal Work & Presentation

* **Post-Newtonian Formalism**
  * Core document on the PN formalism and its regularizations
* **Oral Presentation**
  * Associated slides summarizing the study ([Slides PDF](./assets/GW/PNreg_presentation.pdf))

[<i class="bi bi-file-earmark-pdf"></i> Personal Work PDF](./assets/GW/PNreg.pdf){.btn-surface .c-purple .w-100 style="margin-top: auto; min-height: 40px; height: auto; padding: 8px 12px; font-size: 0.9em;"}
:::
:::

:::
